title: Tach Guard Context
description: The operating-contract packet an external coding agent reads each loop (`tach guard context --json`). It is the machine-readable answer to "what am I allowed to do, what is failing, and what proves I am done" for one guard session. An agent should re-read it before every edit: the allow-lists are the gate, and `done_condition` is the only signal that authorizes claiming the task complete.

goal.description: The goal name resolved from the repo's Tachfile, e.g. FixFailingTests.

run_id.description: The guard session id, e.g. run_….

phase.description: Session phase: open, verified, committed, or aborted.

allowed_files.description: The fs.write globs the agent may edit; edits outside these are rejected at the gate and block commit.

allowed_commands.description: The only commands Tach will run, matched verbatim (no globbing).

forbidden.description: A map of named prohibitions to a human-readable consequence, e.g. out_of_scope_writes / unallowlisted_commands.

current_failure.description: A summary of the most recent failing verify, or null if the last verify passed or none has run.

next_required_action.description: The single next step Tach expects, e.g. "edit files within allowed_files, then run `tach guard verify`".

done_condition.description: The exact check the agent must use to decide it is finished; do not claim done until it holds.

verified.description: True only when every required command passed AND no out-of-scope file changed.
